container_info.description: A real-time web application called 'Insurance Fraud Signal Detection & Investigator Dashboard' on KAVIA.AI. Provides full-stack functionality for uploading and parsing insurance claims, running automated fraud detection rules, managing investigator workflow, and presenting actionable insights. Includes secure CSV upload, risk scoring with visual bands, claims management, and reporting. Built for rapid demo and deployment.
container_info.reasoning: The provided Framework field explicitly lists Supabase and must be used per detection rules. The container is named insurance_db and the Container Type is database; the Dockerfile summary includes multiple DB servers and developer tooling, indicating this container functions as the data/backend storage layer for the Insurance Fraud Signal Detection & Investigator Dashboard. Supabase is a backend-as-a-service built around Postgres and fits the database/platform role for this environment.
alternative_frameworks: Supabase (CLI + local Postgres) -- alternative local deployment modes
requirements: Postgres server (postgresql) - core runtime for Supabase/Postgres database
supabase CLI (supabase) - minimal local control plane for migrations and local dev
pg_restore/pg_dump or psql - basic DB import/export tooling
libpq-dev (or equivalent) - necessary client libraries for connections
python3 (runtime) - for lightweight scripting and automation (already present)
psycopg2-binary or asyncpg (one minimal DB client) - for basic agent DB interactions
nodejs and npm (already present) - required if using Supabase JS client in agents
environment variables configured for DB connection (PGHOST, PGPORT, PGUSER, PGPASSWORD, PGDATABASE)
lightweight migration tool (e.g., pgsimple or supabase migrations via CLI) - minimal schema management
minimal health-check script (simple psql or curl to Supabase REST endpoint) for headless readiness checks
container_info.dockerfile_summary: OS: Ubuntu 24.04 (Debian family), Package Manager: apt-get, Sudo: Present (NOPASSWD), Preinstalled: git, curl, wget, python3, python3-pip, nodejs, npm, build-essential, postgresql, mysql-server, mongodb-org, redis-server, dotnet-sdk-8.0, uvicorn, celery, redis, requests, beautifulsoup4, sphinx, mkdocs, pylint, flask, awscli, boto3, yarn, typescript, @vue/cli, @angular/cli, create-react-app, express-generator, nodemon, pm2, eslint, prettier, webpack, jest

steps.name: Environment: preflight, libpq-dev and Python DB client
steps.description: Validate required system binaries (psql, pg_dump, pg_restore, initdb, pg_ctl, python3, pip). Install libpq-dev if missing. Install psycopg2-binary into system python if project requires it (or asyncpg optionally if project markers exist). Persist supabase CLI PATH if installed, but do not install supabase silently; instead attempt a validated .deb download and verify supabase --version. Operates in the container workspace: /home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/insurance-fraud-detection-dashboard-330-418/insurance_db.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/insurance-fraud-detection-dashboard-330-418/insurance_db"
mkdir -p "$WS"
# required binaries - fail fast if missing
required=(python3 pip3 psql pg_dump pg_restore initdb pg_ctl)
for b in "${required[@]}"; do command -v "$b" >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "fatal: required binary $b missing" >&2; exit 2; }; done
python3 --version
pip3 --version
psql --version
pg_dump --version
# install libpq-dev if missing
if ! dpkg -s libpq-dev >/dev/null 2>&1; then sudo apt-get update -q && sudo DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y -qq libpq-dev; fi
# Python DB client: prefer psycopg2-binary unless project requests asyncpg
if python3 -c "import sys, pkgutil, pathlib; print()" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  if [ -f "$WS/requirements.txt" ] && grep -Eqi "asyncpg" "$WS/requirements.txt"; then
    sudo python3 -m pip install --upgrade --no-input asyncpg || { echo "error: asyncpg install failed" >&2; exit 11; }
  else
    if ! python3 -c "import psycopg2" >/dev/null 2>&1; then sudo python3 -m pip install --upgrade --no-input psycopg2-binary || { echo "error: psycopg2 install failed" >&2; exit 12; }; fi
  fi
fi
python3 -c "import psycopg2 as p; print('psycopg2', getattr(p,'__version__','unknown'))" || true
# Supabase CLI: attempt validated .deb install only if requested by caller via SUPABASE_INSTALL=1
if [ "${SUPABASE_INSTALL:-0}" = "1" ] && ! command -v supabase >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  ARCH=$(dpkg --print-architecture 2>/dev/null || echo amd64)
  case "$ARCH" in amd64) DEB_ARCH=amd64;; arm64|aarch64) DEB_ARCH=arm64;; *) DEB_ARCH=amd64;; esac
  URL="https://github.com/supabase/cli/releases/latest/download/supabase_${DEB_ARCH}.deb"
  TMPDEB=$(mktemp -u "/tmp/supabase_${DEB_ARCH}.XXXX.deb")
  if curl -fsSL -o "$TMPDEB" "$URL"; then
    sudo dpkg -i "$TMPDEB" || sudo apt-get install -y -f || { echo "error: dpkg/apt fix failed installing supabase" >&2; exit 13; }
    rm -f "$TMPDEB"
    command -v supabase >/dev/null 2>&1 && supabase --version || { echo "warning: supabase installed but --version failed" >&2; }
  else
    echo "note: supabase .deb not found at $URL; skip install" >&2
  fi
fi
# persist supabase path if present (expanded path written)
if command -v supabase >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  SUP_PATH=$(command -v supabase)
  SUP_DIR=$(dirname "$SUP_PATH")
  echo "export PATH=\"$SUP_DIR:\$PATH\"" | sudo tee /etc/profile.d/supabase_path.sh >/dev/null
  sudo chmod 644 /etc/profile.d/supabase_path.sh
fi

output_scripts.name: install
output_scripts.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/insurance-fraud-detection-dashboard-330-418/insurance_db"
mkdir -p "$WS"
# required binaries - fail fast if missing
required=(python3 pip3 psql pg_dump pg_restore initdb pg_ctl)
for b in "${required[@]}"; do command -v "$b" >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "fatal: required binary $b missing" >&2; exit 2; }; done
python3 --version || true
pip3 --version || true
psql --version || true
pg_dump --version || true
# install libpq-dev if missing
if ! dpkg -s libpq-dev >/dev/null 2>&1; then sudo apt-get update -q && sudo DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y -qq libpq-dev; fi
# Python DB client: prefer psycopg2-binary unless project requests asyncpg
# detect marker in requirements.txt in workspace
if [ -f "$WS/requirements.txt" ] && grep -Eqi "asyncpg" "$WS/requirements.txt"; then
  if ! python3 -c "import asyncpg" >/dev/null 2>&1; then sudo python3 -m pip install --upgrade --no-input asyncpg || { echo "error: asyncpg install failed" >&2; exit 11; }; fi
else
  if ! python3 -c "import psycopg2" >/dev/null 2>&1; then sudo python3 -m pip install --upgrade --no-input psycopg2-binary || { echo "error: psycopg2 install failed" >&2; exit 12; }; fi
fi
python3 -c "import importlib,sys
try:
  if importlib.util.find_spec('psycopg2'):
    import psycopg2 as p; print('psycopg2', getattr(p,'__version__','unknown'))
  elif importlib.util.find_spec('asyncpg'):
    import asyncpg as a; print('asyncpg', getattr(a,'__version__','unknown'))
  else:
    print('no-db-client-found')
except Exception as e:
  print('db-client-check-failed', e)
" || true
# Supabase CLI: attempt validated .deb install only if SUPABASE_INSTALL=1
if [ "${SUPABASE_INSTALL:-0}" = "1" ] && ! command -v supabase >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  ARCH=$(dpkg --print-architecture 2>/dev/null || echo amd64)
  case "$ARCH" in amd64) DEB_ARCH=amd64;; arm64|aarch64) DEB_ARCH=arm64;; *) DEB_ARCH=amd64;; esac
  URL="https://github.com/supabase/cli/releases/latest/download/supabase_${DEB_ARCH}.deb"
  TMPDEB=$(mktemp -u "/tmp/supabase_${DEB_ARCH}.XXXX.deb")
  if curl -fsSL -o "$TMPDEB" "$URL"; then
    sudo dpkg -i "$TMPDEB" || sudo apt-get install -y -f || { echo "error: dpkg/apt fix failed installing supabase" >&2; rm -f "$TMPDEB"; exit 13; }
    rm -f "$TMPDEB"
    if command -v supabase >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      supabase --version || { echo "warning: supabase installed but --version failed" >&2; }
    else
      echo "warning: supabase binary not found after install" >&2
    fi
  else
    echo "note: supabase .deb not found at $URL; skip install" >&2
  fi
fi
# persist supabase path if present (expanded path written)
if command -v supabase >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  SUP_PATH=$(command -v supabase)
  SUP_DIR=$(dirname "$SUP_PATH")
  echo "export PATH=\"$SUP_DIR:\$PATH\"" | sudo tee /etc/profile.d/supabase_path.sh >/dev/null
  sudo chmod 644 /etc/profile.d/supabase_path.sh
fi
exit 0

steps.summary: Executed environment preflight actions as a single idempotent install script placed in .init/install.sh. The script validates presence of required binaries (python3, pip3, psql, pg_dump, pg_restore, initdb, pg_ctl), prints versions, installs libpq-dev if missing, installs psycopg2-binary into system Python (or asyncpg if requirements.txt contains asyncpg), and conditionally attempts a validated supabase .deb install only when SUPABASE_INSTALL=1 is set by the caller. If supabase is present it persists the expanded supabase directory to /etc/profile.d/supabase_path.sh. The script follows non-interactive, strict error handling and uses the authoritative workspace path. It is idempotent and uses sudo for privileged operations. Any failures produce clear exit codes and messages. No architect help required.
steps.qa_summary: The engineer reported a clear, idempotent install script (.init/install.sh) that validated required binaries (python3, pip3, psql, pg_dump, pg_restore, initdb, pg_ctl), printed versions, installed libpq-dev if missing, installed psycopg2-binary (or asyncpg when detected), and conditionally handled supabase .deb installation only when SUPABASE_INSTALL=1. Script adheres to strict error handling, uses sudo for privileged ops, persists supabase PATH to /etc/profile.d when present, and provides clear exit codes/messages. No critical errors or security issues (e.g., hardcoded credentials) were reported. Core objective of the environment preflight step is complete.

steps.name: Configuration: non-sensitive PG env template and guidance
steps.description: Create an /etc/profile.d template that exports non-sensitive PG environment variables (PGHOST, PGPORT, PGUSER, PGDATABASE) but NOT PGPASSWORD. This provides a safe global template that developers/agents can copy and fill with secrets at runtime. Operates in workspace /home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/insurance-fraud-detection-dashboard-330-418/insurance_db.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/insurance-fraud-detection-dashboard-330-418/insurance_db"
# write a safe template (no secrets) - idempotent
sudo tee /etc/profile.d/pg_env_template.sh >/dev/null <<'EOF'
# PG env template (fill PGPASSWORD via secure agent at runtime; do NOT store secrets here)
export PGHOST="localhost"
export PGPORT="5432"
export PGUSER="postgres"
export PGDATABASE="postgres"
# export PGPASSWORD should be provided at runtime, e.g. via agent environment injection
EOF
sudo chmod 644 /etc/profile.d/pg_env_template.sh
# also write a README in workspace
mkdir -p "$WS"
cat > "$WS/README.env" <<'TXT'
This template is available at /etc/profile.d/pg_env_template.sh (non-sensitive). Do NOT put PGPASSWORD there.
To run commands non-interactively, supply PGPASSWORD at runtime, e.g.:
PGPASSWORD=secret ./scripts/migrate.sh
Or export PGPASSWORD for the session (agent injection) and source /etc/profile.d/pg_env_template.sh
TXT

steps.name: Scaffolding: create migrations, scripts (migrate, healthcheck, start/stop)
steps.description: Create workspace scaffolding under the authoritative workspace path with a sample SQL migration, scripts/migrate.sh (psql fallback by default unless a clear supabase config exists), healthcheck.sh, start-db.sh and stop-db.sh. Ephemeral clusters use mktemp for unique data dirs, write a permissive pg_hba.conf for testing, and clean up on exit. Operates in workspace /home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/insurance-fraud-detection-dashboard-330-418/insurance_db.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/insurance-fraud-detection-dashboard-330-418/insurance_db"
mkdir -p "$WS/migrations" "$WS/scripts"
cat > "$WS/migrations/0001_init.sql" <<'SQL'
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS test_table (id serial PRIMARY KEY, created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now());
SQL
# migrate helper: prefer psql fallback unless explicit supabase config exists
cat > "$WS/scripts/migrate.sh" <<'BASH'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="${WS}"
: "${PGHOST:?PGHOST must be set (e.g. localhost)}"
: "${PGUSER:?PGUSER must be set}"
: "${PGDATABASE:?PGDATABASE must be set}"
PGPORT="${PGPORT:-5432}"
# detect clear supabase config
if command -v supabase >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ -d "$WS/supabase" ] && [ -f "$WS/supabase/config.toml" ]; then
  cd "$WS" && supabase db push --skip-generate || { echo "supabase db push failed" >&2; exit 4; }
  exit 0
fi
# psql fallback
if ! command -v psql >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo "error: psql not found" >&2; exit 5; fi
if [ -n "${PGPASSWORD:-}" ]; then PGPASSWORD="$PGPASSWORD" psql -h "$PGHOST" -p "$PGPORT" -U "$PGUSER" -d "$PGDATABASE" -f "$WS/migrations/0001_init.sql"; else psql -h "$PGHOST" -p "$PGPORT" -U "$PGUSER" -d "$PGDATABASE" -f "$WS/migrations/0001_init.sql"; fi
BASH
chmod +x "$WS/scripts/migrate.sh"
# healthcheck
cat > "$WS/scripts/healthcheck.sh" <<'BASH'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
: "${PGHOST:?}"
: "${PGUSER:?}"
: "${PGDATABASE:?}"
PGPORT="${PGPORT:-5432}"
if ! command -v psql >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo "error: psql not found" >&2; exit 6; fi
if [ -n "${PGPASSWORD:-}" ]; then PGPASSWORD="$PGPASSWORD" psql -h "$PGHOST" -p "$PGPORT" -U "$PGUSER" -d "$PGDATABASE" -c '\q' >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo OK || { echo DOWN; exit 7; }; else psql -h "$PGHOST" -p "$PGPORT" -U "$PGUSER" -d "$PGDATABASE" -c '\q' >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo OK || { echo DOWN; exit 8; }; fi
BASH
chmod +x "$WS/scripts/healthcheck.sh"
# start-db helper: systemd allowed only if PID1 is systemd and unit exists; fallback is ephemeral initdb with secure defaults
cat > "$WS/scripts/start-db.sh" <<'BASH'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
# strong systemd guard
if [ -d /run/systemd/system ] && [ "$(ps -p 1 -o comm=)" = "systemd" ] && command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1 && systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q postgresql; then
  sudo systemctl start postgresql && echo systemd_started && exit 0
fi
# ephemeral initdb fallback
if ! command -v initdb >/dev/null 2>&1 || ! command -v pg_ctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo "error: initdb/pg_ctl required for fallback" >&2; exit 9; fi
DATADIR=$(mktemp -d "/tmp/pgdata.XXXX")
trap 'pg_ctl -D "$DATADIR" -w stop >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; rm -rf "$DATADIR"' EXIT
initdb -D "$DATADIR"
# allow local trust for ephemeral testing only
echo "local all all trust" > "$DATADIR/pg_hba.conf"
pg_ctl -D "$DATADIR" -o "-c listen_addresses=localhost" -w start
echo "ephemeral:$DATADIR"
BASH
chmod +x "$WS/scripts/start-db.sh"
# stop-db helper
cat > "$WS/scripts/stop-db.sh" <<'BASH'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
# systemd stop if unit exists
if [ -d /run/systemd/system ] && [ "$(ps -p 1 -o comm=)" = "systemd" ] && command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1 && systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q postgresql; then
  sudo systemctl stop postgresql || true; exit 0
fi
# for ephemeral clusters, user must supply DATADIR or the helper will no-op
if [ -n "${DATADIR:-}" ] && [ -d "$DATADIR" ]; then pg_ctl -D "$DATADIR" -w stop || true; rm -rf "$DATADIR" || true; fi
BASH
chmod +x "$WS/scripts/stop-db.sh"

steps.name: Dependencies: install pytest (apt preferred) and validate Python DB client
steps.description: Install pytest via apt if available; otherwise install via pip into system python. Validate that psycopg2-binary or asyncpg is importable. Operates in workspace /home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/insurance-fraud-detection-dashboard-330-418/insurance_db.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/insurance-fraud-detection-dashboard-330-418/insurance_db"
# prefer apt package for pytest for system-managed stability
if ! dpkg -s python3-pytest >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  if apt-cache show python3-pytest >/dev/null 2>&1; then sudo apt-get update -q && sudo DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y -qq python3-pytest; else
    sudo python3 -m pip install --upgrade --no-input pytest || { echo "error: pytest pip install failed" >&2; exit 17; }
  fi
fi
python3 -c "import pytest; print('pytest', getattr(pytest,'__version__','unknown'))"
# verify DB client present
if ! python3 -c "import psycopg2" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  if python3 -c "import asyncpg" >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo "asyncpg present"; else echo "error: neither psycopg2 nor asyncpg available" >&2; exit 18; fi
fi

output_scripts.name: install
output_scripts.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/insurance-fraud-detection-dashboard-330-418/insurance_db"
# ensure libpq-dev (headers for psycopg2)
if ! dpkg -s libpq-dev >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  sudo apt-get update -q && sudo DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y -qq libpq-dev
fi
# install pytest: prefer apt package for system-managed stability, else pip
if ! dpkg -s python3-pytest >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  if apt-cache show python3-pytest >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    sudo apt-get update -q && sudo DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y -qq python3-pytest
  else
    sudo python3 -m pip install --upgrade --no-input pytest || { echo "error: pytest pip install failed" >&2; exit 17; }
  fi
fi
# report pytest version
python3 -c "import pytest; print('pytest', getattr(pytest,'__version__','unknown'))"
# verify Python DB client: prefer psycopg2, fall back to asyncpg; install psycopg2-binary if neither present
if python3 -c "import psycopg2" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  python3 -c "import psycopg2; print('psycopg2', getattr(psycopg2,'__version__','unknown'))"
else
  if python3 -c "import asyncpg" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    python3 -c "import asyncpg; print('asyncpg', getattr(asyncpg,'__version__','unknown'))"
  else
    # attempt to install psycopg2-binary into system python
    sudo python3 -m pip install --upgrade --no-input psycopg2-binary || { echo "error: failed to install psycopg2-binary" >&2; exit 18; }
    python3 -c "import psycopg2; print('psycopg2', getattr(psycopg2,'__version__','unknown'))" || { echo "error: psycopg2 import failed after install" >&2; exit 19; }
  fi
fi
# print basic tool versions for traceability
command -v psql >/dev/null 2>&1 && psql --version || echo "psql not found"
python3 --version || true
python3 -m pip --version || true

steps.name: Testing: create pytest that validates DB connectivity (skip when creds absent)
steps.description: Create a pytest that attempts to connect using psycopg2 (or asyncpg skipped) but will skip if required PG env vars are not present. Run pytest and report clear exit codes and summary. Operates in workspace /home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/insurance-fraud-detection-dashboard-330-418/insurance_db.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/insurance-fraud-detection-dashboard-330-418/insurance_db"
mkdir -p "$WS/tests"
cat > "$WS/tests/test_db_connection.py" <<'PY'
import os
import pytest
try:
    import psycopg2
except Exception:
    pytest.skip('psycopg2 not available')
PGHOST = os.getenv('PGHOST')
PGUSER = os.getenv('PGUSER')
PGDATABASE = os.getenv('PGDATABASE')
if not (PGHOST and PGUSER and PGDATABASE):
    pytest.skip('PGHOST, PGUSER and PGDATABASE must be set to run DB connectivity test')
PGPORT = int(os.getenv('PGPORT', '5432'))
PGPASSWORD = os.getenv('PGPASSWORD', '')

def test_connect():
    conn = None
    try:
        conn = psycopg2.connect(host=PGHOST, port=PGPORT, user=PGUSER, password=PGPASSWORD or None, dbname=PGDATABASE)
        cur = conn.cursor()
        cur.execute('SELECT 1')
        assert cur.fetchone()[0] == 1
    finally:
        if conn:
            conn.close()
PY
# run tests; capture exit code and summarize
python3 -m pytest -q "$WS/tests/test_db_connection.py" || rc=$? && rc=${rc:-0}
if [ "${rc:-0}" -ne 0 ]; then echo "tests: failures (exit $rc)" >&2; exit $rc; else echo "tests: OK"; fi

output_scripts.name: test
output_scripts.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/insurance-fraud-detection-dashboard-330-418/insurance_db"
mkdir -p "$WS/tests"
cat > "$WS/tests/test_db_connection.py" <<'PY'
import os
import pytest

# Prefer skipping tests at collection using pytest.importorskip and pytest.mark.skipif
psycopg2 = pytest.importorskip('psycopg2')

PGHOST = os.getenv('PGHOST')
PGUSER = os.getenv('PGUSER')
PGDATABASE = os.getenv('PGDATABASE')

skip_no_creds = not (PGHOST and PGUSER and PGDATABASE)

@pytest.mark.skipif(skip_no_creds, reason='PGHOST, PGUSER and PGDATABASE must be set to run DB connectivity test')
def test_connect():
    PGPORT = int(os.getenv('PGPORT', '5432'))
    PGPASSWORD = os.getenv('PGPASSWORD', '')
    conn = None
    try:
        conn = psycopg2.connect(host=PGHOST, port=PGPORT, user=PGUSER, password=PGPASSWORD or None, dbname=PGDATABASE)
        cur = conn.cursor()
        cur.execute('SELECT 1')
        assert cur.fetchone()[0] == 1
    finally:
        if conn:
            conn.close()
PY
# run tests; capture exit code and summarize
python3 -m pytest -q "$WS/tests/test_db_connection.py" || rc=$? && rc=${rc:-0}
if [ "${rc:-0}" -ne 0 ]; then echo "tests: failures (exit $rc)" >&2; exit $rc; else echo "tests: OK"; fi

output_scripts.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/insurance-fraud-detection-dashboard-330-418/insurance_db"
mkdir -p "$WS/tests"
cat > "$WS/tests/test_db_connection.py" <<'PY'
import os
import pytest
try:
    import psycopg2
except Exception:
    pytest.skip('psycopg2 not available')
PGHOST = os.getenv('PGHOST')
PGUSER = os.getenv('PGUSER')
PGDATABASE = os.getenv('PGDATABASE')
if not (PGHOST and PGUSER and PGDATABASE):
    pytest.skip('PGHOST, PGUSER and PGDATABASE must be set to run DB connectivity test')
PGPORT = int(os.getenv('PGPORT', '5432'))
PGPASSWORD = os.getenv('PGPASSWORD', '')

def test_connect():
    conn = None
    try:
        conn = psycopg2.connect(host=PGHOST, port=PGPORT, user=PGUSER, password=PGPASSWORD or None, dbname=PGDATABASE)
        cur = conn.cursor()
        cur.execute('SELECT 1')
        assert cur.fetchone()[0] == 1
    finally:
        if conn:
            conn.close()
PY

# Ensure pytest is available (use system apt if available else pip)
if ! command -v pytest >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y -qq python3-pytest || true
  fi
fi

# Ensure psycopg2-binary importable, install via pip if not
python3 - <<'PY'
try:
    import psycopg2
except Exception:
    import sys, subprocess
    subprocess.check_call([sys.executable, '-m', 'pip', 'install', '--quiet', 'psycopg2-binary'])
PY

# Run the single pytest file and capture exit code and concise summary
python3 -m pytest -q "$WS/tests/test_db_connection.py"
rc=$?
if [ "$rc" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "tests: OK"
  exit 0
else
  echo "tests: failures (exit $rc)" >&2
  exit $rc
fi

steps.name: Validation: start DB (safe), run migrations, healthcheck, tests and capture evidence
steps.description: Validation sequence: start Postgres safely (systemd only if PID1 is systemd and postgresql unit exists), otherwise start ephemeral initdb cluster with unique DATADIR and permissive pg_hba.conf for testing. Requires PGHOST, PGUSER, PGDATABASE (PGPASSWORD optional) provided by caller or via /etc/profile.d/pg_env_template.sh + runtime secret. Runs migrate.sh, healthcheck.sh, pytest and prints concise evidence including tool versions and psql \dt output. Ensures cleanup of ephemeral clusters it created. Operates in workspace /home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/insurance-fraud-detection-dashboard-330-418/insurance_db.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/insurance-fraud-detection-dashboard-330-418/insurance_db"
: "${PGHOST:?PGHOST must be set (e.g. localhost)}"
: "${PGUSER:?PGUSER must be set}"
: "${PGDATABASE:?PGDATABASE must be set}"
PGPORT="${PGPORT:-5432}"
STARTED_BY_SCRIPT=0
# evidence: versions
psql --version || true; pg_dump --version || true; python3 --version || true; pip3 --version || true
if command -v supabase >/dev/null 2>&1; then supabase --version || true; fi
# try systemd start only when safe
if [ -d /run/systemd/system ] && [ "$(ps -p 1 -o comm=)" = "systemd" ] && command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1 && systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q postgresql; then
  if systemctl --quiet is-active postgresql; then echo "postgresql: systemd active"; else sudo systemctl start postgresql && STARTED_BY_SCRIPT=1; fi
else
  # ephemeral fallback
  DATADIR=$(mktemp -d "/tmp/pgdata.XXXX")
  trap 'pg_ctl -D "$DATADIR" -w stop >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; rm -rf "$DATADIR"' EXIT
  initdb -D "$DATADIR"
  echo "local all all trust" > "$DATADIR/pg_hba.conf"
  pg_ctl -D "$DATADIR" -o "-c listen_addresses=localhost" -w start
  STARTED_BY_SCRIPT=2
  # override PGHOST/PGPORT for ephemeral cluster
  export PGHOST=${PGHOST:-localhost}
  export PGPORT=${PGPORT:-5432}
fi
# run migrations
"$WS/scripts/migrate.sh"
# run healthcheck
if "$WS/scripts/healthcheck.sh" | grep -q OK; then echo "healthcheck: OK"; else echo "healthcheck: FAILED" >&2; fi
# run tests and capture result
if python3 -m pytest -q "$WS/tests/test_db_connection.py"; then echo "pytest: OK"; else echo "pytest: failures or skipped"; fi
# evidence: list tables
if [ -n "${PGPASSWORD:-}" ]; then PGPASSWORD="$PGPASSWORD" psql -h "$PGHOST" -p "$PGPORT" -U "$PGUSER" -d "$PGDATABASE" -c "\dt" || true; else psql -h "$PGHOST" -p "$PGPORT" -U "$PGUSER" -d "$PGDATABASE" -c "\dt" || true; fi
# cleanup handled by trap for ephemeral; stop systemd if we started it
if [ "$STARTED_BY_SCRIPT" -eq 1 ]; then sudo systemctl stop postgresql || true; fi
echo "validation: completed"

output_scripts.name: start
output_scripts.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/insurance-fraud-detection-dashboard-330-418/insurance_db"
: "${PGHOST:?PGHOST must be set (e.g. localhost)}"
PGPORT="${PGPORT:-5432}"
STARTED_BY_SCRIPT=0
if [ -d /run/systemd/system ] && [ "$(ps -p 1 -o comm=)" = "systemd" ] && command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1 && systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q postgresql; then
  if systemctl --quiet is-active postgresql; then echo "postgresql: systemd active"; else sudo systemctl start postgresql && STARTED_BY_SCRIPT=1; fi
else
  DATADIR=$(mktemp -d "/tmp/pgdata.XXXX")
  trap 'pg_ctl -D "$DATADIR" -w stop >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; rm -rf "$DATADIR"' EXIT
  initdb -D "$DATADIR"
  echo "local all all trust" > "$DATADIR/pg_hba.conf"
  pg_ctl -D "$DATADIR" -o "-c listen_addresses=localhost -c unix_socket_directories=/tmp" -w start
  STARTED_BY_SCRIPT=2
  export PGHOST="${PGHOST:-localhost}"
  export PGPORT="${PGPORT:-5432}"
  # export DATADIR for other scripts
  echo "$DATADIR" > "$WS/.init/ephemeral_pg_datadir" || true
fi
# record start marker
mkdir -p "$WS/.init" && printf "%s" "$STARTED_BY_SCRIPT" > "$WS/.init/start_marker"

output_scripts.name: stop
output_scripts.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/insurance-fraud-detection-dashboard-330-418/insurance_db"
if [ -f "$WS/.init/start_marker" ]; then
  STARTED_BY_SCRIPT=$(cat "$WS/.init/start_marker" || echo "0")
else
  STARTED_BY_SCRIPT=0
fi
if [ "$STARTED_BY_SCRIPT" -eq 1 ]; then
  sudo systemctl stop postgresql || true
elif [ "$STARTED_BY_SCRIPT" -eq 2 ]; then
  if [ -f "$WS/.init/ephemeral_pg_datadir" ]; then
    DATADIR=$(cat "$WS/.init/ephemeral_pg_datadir")
    pg_ctl -D "$DATADIR" -w stop || true
    rm -rf "$DATADIR" || true
    rm -f "$WS/.init/ephemeral_pg_datadir" || true
  fi
fi
rm -f "$WS/.init/start_marker" || true

output_scripts.name: migrate
output_scripts.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WS="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/insurance-fraud-detection-dashboard-330-418/insurance_db"
# simple migration: create schema_migrations table and sample table if not exists
: "${PGHOST:?PGHOST must be set}"
: "${PGUSER:?PGUSER must be set}"
: "${PGDATABASE:?PGDATABASE must be set}"
PGPORT="${PGPORT:-5432}"
SQL="CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_migrations(id serial primary key, version text not null, applied_at timestamptz default now());\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sample_table(id serial primary key, name text);
"
if [ -n "${PGPASSWORD:-}" ]; then PGPASSWORD="$PGPASSWORD" psql -h "$PGHOST" -p "$PGPORT" -U "$PGUSER" -d "$PGDATABASE" -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 -q -c "$SQL"; else psql -h "$PGHOST" -p "$PGPORT" -U "$PGUSER" -d "$PGDATABASE" -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 -q -c "$SQL"; fi

: "${PGHOST:?PGHOST must be set}"

: "${PGDATABASE:?PGDATABASE must be set}"
PGPORT="${PGPORT:-5432}"
if [ -n "${PGPASSWORD:-}" ]; then
  PGPASSWORD="$PGPASSWORD" psql -h "$PGHOST" -p "$PGPORT" -U "$PGUSER" -d "$PGDATABASE" -c "SELECT 1;" -q >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "OK" || (echo "FAIL" && exit 1)
else

fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash

    cur=conn.cursor()
    cur.execute('SELECT 1')
    print('DB_TEST: OK')
    cur.close(); conn.close()
except Exception as e:
    print('DB_TEST: FAIL', e); sys.exit(2)
PY

reasoning: Reworked the original plan to address analyst CRITICAL/HIGH feedback: all scripts now strictly use the authoritative workspace (/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/insurance-fraud-detection-dashboard-330-418/insurance_db). Preflight checks fail fast on missing required binaries. Supabase CLI install logic is hardened: HTTP download is validated, dpkg errors are not suppressed, install is verified by running `supabase --version`, and the write to /etc/profile.d persists the expanded SUP_DIR. Systemctl usage is strongly guarded: scripts only attempt systemctl when PID 1 is systemd and the postgresql unit exists. Ephemeral initdb clusters use mktemp-d for unique TMPDIR, configure pg_hba.conf minimally to allow local connections for tests (trust for ephemeral test runs only), and are cleaned up on exit. A new configuration step writes a non-sensitive /etc/profile.d/pg_env_template.sh that exports PGHOST, PGPORT, PGUSER, PGDATABASE (no PGPASSWORD) to satisfy requirement #8 safely and documents how to supply PGPASSWORD at runtime. Dependencies step prefers the OS package for pytest but falls back to pip when necessary; installs target system python using sudo python3 -m pip. Test and validation scripts capture pytest exit codes and report clear outcomes; validation starts/stops DB instances deterministically and emits evidence (tool versions and \dt). Scripts were shortened where reasonable, but safety and correctness were prioritized. Optional asyncpg install is supported if a project marker requests it. Overall the plan prioritizes idempotency, non-interactive execution, safe environment persistence, and clear failure modes for automated agents.

change_logs.message: Refined plan to address analyst feedback: env-001: hardening supabase CLI install (architecture check, dpkg -> apt -f with -y, version validation), avoid writing export PATH="$PATH" and instead persist only the directory containing the supabase binary; install libpq-dev conditionally and verify psycopg2-binary post-install. scaffold-001: fixed psql invocation to use explicit flags and require PG* env vars (fail-fast), changed start/stop helper to prefer pg_ctl/initdb fallback when systemctl is unavailable and removed insecure persistence of credentials. dependencies-001: ensured pytest and psycopg2 validation are performed. test-001: updated pytest to skip when required env vars absent and to normalize PGPORT handling. validation-001: removed unsafe default quoting for PGPASSWORD, added detection for systemctl and pg_ctl fallback to start a temporary postgres instance for validation, ensured migrate.sh chooses deterministic psql fallback by default unless a valid supabase project exists, and added explicit verification steps for installed tools. Adjusted script_names to be unique and aligned step ordering and execution_priority. These changes address critical security issues (no credentials persisted), robustness (systemctl fallback), correctness (psql flags and quoting), and observability (fail-fast, version checks).

Refined plan to address analyst feedback: fixed incorrect workspace variable in dependencies-001; added explicit preflight checks for psql/pg_dump/pg_restore/pg_ctl/initdb/python3/pip in env-001; made pip installs (psycopg2-binary, pytest) explicit with sudo targeting system Python and visible post-install version checks; improved supabase CLI install logic by mapping dpkg architectures to common release asset names and added clear fallback/error messages; changed /etc/profile.d writes to use sudo tee with a quoted heredoc for atomic, safe writes; added detection to ensure systemctl only used when container is systemd-enabled to avoid host interference; added explicit checks and surfaced stderr for initdb/pg_ctl failures; added workspace README helper showing how to pass PG* env vars non-interactively; relaxed excessive output suppression for critical verification commands. Modified steps: env-001 (heavy edits), scaffold-001 (added tool checks and surfaced errors), dependencies-001 (fixed WS path and pip invocation), test-001 (minor clarity), validation-001 (added version evidence, systemd detection, tool checks). These changes address CRITICAL and HIGH recommendations (workspace consistency, CLI presence validation, robust pip installs, safe /etc/profile.d writes, supabase asset mapping, systemctl safety) and several MEDIUM/LOW suggestions for traceability and usability.
